_comment: sdlc-skills skill registry — canonical source for which skills exist and how to fetch them. Skills in the sdlc-skills monorepo carry `monorepo: sdlc-skills` + `name: <subdir>`. Third-party skill repos carry `repo: owner/repo` (+ optional `ref`, + optional `subdir` for multi-skill repos) and are fetched by `bin/init.mjs` via git clone + subdir symlink. The Octobots supervisor's install.sh delegates all content resolution to this installer.

skills.name: browser-verify
skills.description: Browser verification via CDP — visual checks, screenshots, accessibility

skills.name: code-review
skills.description: Structured code review — security, quality, conventions

skills.name: git-workflow
skills.description: Git branching, PR creation, and merge workflow

skills.name: issue-tracking
skills.description: GitHub issue lifecycle — labelling, commenting, closing

skills.name: playwright-testing
skills.description: End-to-end browser testing with Playwright

skills.name: bugfix-workflow
skills.description: End-to-end bugfix workflow — reproduce, test, fix, verify, document

skills.name: xray-testing
skills.description: CRUD + results import on Xray entities (Test / Precondition / Test Set / Test Plan / Test Execution / Test Run) across Xray Cloud (GraphQL) and Server/DC (REST). JUnit / Cucumber / Xray JSON import formats

skills.name: atlassian-content
skills.description: Create well-formatted Jira issues/comments (ADF, API v3) and Confluence pages (storage format) with accountId-backed mentions and mandatory post-creation re-fetch + repair

skills.name: tosca-automation
skills.description: Tricentis TOSCA Cloud full lifecycle — create/update/run TestCases, Modules (Html + SapEngine), Reusable Blocks, Playlists, Inventory folders, TSU import/export. Bundled `tosca_cli.py` (httpx + typer + rich)

skills.name: test-case-analysis
skills.description: Execute a TMS test case against the live app, capture stable selectors, flag defects, and emit an Automation-Friendly Spec (AFS). Used by qa-engineer

skills.name: test-automation-workflow
skills.description: End-to-end test automation workflow — explore, specify (AFS), implement, review. Pluggable TMS adapters (Zephyr Scale, TestRail, Xray, Azure Test Plans, markdown) over HTTP or MCP

skills.name: implement-feature
skills.description: End-to-end feature implementation workflow — plan, test, build, ship

skills.name: plan-feature
skills.description: Structured feature planning — requirements, feasibility, acceptance criteria

skills.name: project-seeder
skills.description: Generate AGENTS.md, .agents/ content docs, and per-role memory briefings for a new project (used by scout)

skills.name: deep-research
skills.description: Deep research workflow — trends, topic analysis, fact-checking

skills.name: context-gatherer
skills.description: Cross-channel context gathering — email, Teams, local KB

skills.name: goal-verifier
skills.description: Goal-backward verification — check outcomes, not task completion

skills.name: memory
skills.description: Persistent file-based memory across conversations

skills.name: obsidian-vault
skills.description: Read/write the user's Obsidian second brain

skills.name: msgraph
skills.description: Microsoft Graph (email/calendar/Teams) integration
SwiftUI code review and authoring — modern APIs, performance, accessibility (by Paul Hudson)
SwiftData code review and authoring — modern APIs, CloudKit compatibility (by Paul Hudson)
Swift Testing code review and authoring — modern APIs (by Paul Hudson)
Swift concurrency code review and authoring — actors, async/await, structured concurrency (by Paul Hudson)
Test-driven development — vertical-slice tracer bullets, integration-style tests, interface design for testability (by Matt Pocock)
Ideation before coding — explore the problem space before committing to a solution (from obra/superpowers)
Root-cause tracing, condition-based waiting, defense-in-depth for reproducing and fixing bugs (from obra/superpowers)
Pre-handoff check — verify the work against the spec before calling it done (from obra/superpowers)
Meta-skill for authoring SKILL.md files — structure, triggers, and verification (from obra/superpowers)
How to hand off work for review — prepare context, state scope, make the reviewer's job easy (from obra/superpowers)
How to process review feedback — verify technically, don't perform agreement (from obra/superpowers)

Drive Playwright from the command line — launch browsers, navigate, interact via snapshots/locators, manage tabs and storage, mock network, record traces, and generate Playwright tests (by Microsoft Playwright)
